intro.title: Grammar of Graphics
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Grammar of Graphics is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
library(ggplot2)
print("Data + aesthetics + geoms + scales + theme")

challenges.title: Easy: Recognize Grammar of Graphics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code related to **Grammar of Graphics**. Include: `ggplot2`.

challenges.title: Medium: Complete the pattern
challenges.promptMarkdown: Complete code that demonstrates **Grammar of Graphics**.

challenges.title: Hard: Apply the concept
challenges.promptMarkdown: Apply **Grammar of Graphics** in a short R snippet.

questions.prompt: What best describes Grammar of Graphics?

choices.label: A core R concept covered in this topic

questions.prompt: Name one R keyword or function from this topic.
This topic is important in R because:
